text: The video shows a laparoscopic technique for mesh removal using a TAPP approach. The surgeon carefully dissects the peritoneum, revealing a distorted anatomy with an obliterated reoperative plane. An old mesh plug is visible, adherent to the spermatic cord, making it challenging to distinguish the interface between the mesh and the cord.

The robotic technique is demonstrated for the removal of a clam-shelled TEP mesh causing chronic pain. The anatomy is challenging to visualize, requiring careful navigation around the vessels. Anteriorly, the epigastric vessels are visible, while posteriorly, there is a risk of injuring the iliac vessels. The bladder and bowel are also visible and pose potential threats during the procedure.
